gov_controls.title: Access Control Policy
gov_controls.description: Organization maintains a formal access control policy that defines roles, responsibilities, and procedures for granting, modifying, and revoking logical and physical access to systems and data.

gov_controls.title: Encryption at Rest
gov_controls.description: All sensitive data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 or equivalent. Encryption keys are managed through a centralized key management service with automatic rotation.

gov_controls.title: Incident Response Plan
gov_controls.description: Organization maintains a documented incident response plan covering identification, containment, eradication, recovery, and lessons learned. Plan is tested via tabletop exercises semi-annually.

gov_controls.title: Change Management Process
gov_controls.description: All changes to production systems require documented approval, testing in staging, rollback plan, and post-deployment verification. Emergency changes require retroactive approval within 24 hours.

gov_controls.title: Data Subject Rights Handling
gov_controls.description: Organization has processes to handle data subject access requests (DSAR), right to erasure, data portability, and consent withdrawal within GDPR mandated timelines.

gov_controls.title: Business Continuity Plan
gov_controls.description: Organization maintains business continuity and disaster recovery plans with defined RTO (4 hours) and RPO (1 hour). Plans are tested annually via full simulation exercises.

gov_controls.title: Security Event Monitoring
gov_controls.description: Continuous monitoring of security events via SIEM with automated alerting for anomalous access patterns, failed authentication attempts, and privilege escalation. 24/7 on-call rotation.

gov_audit_findings.title: Incomplete access review for terminated employees
gov_audit_findings.description: 3 of 12 sampled terminated employees retained system access for 5-15 days after termination date. Access revocation SLA of 24 hours was not met.
gov_audit_findings.remediation_plan: Implement automated access revocation triggered by HR system termination event. Add daily reconciliation check between HR system and IAM.

gov_audit_findings.title: Incident response plan not tested in 8 months
gov_audit_findings.description: The IR plan requires semi-annual tabletop exercises. Last exercise was conducted 8 months ago, exceeding the 6-month requirement.
gov_audit_findings.remediation_plan: Scheduled tabletop exercise for Feb 2026. Added recurring calendar events for semi-annual exercises with automated reminders.

gov_audit_findings.title: DSAR response time exceeds 30-day GDPR requirement
gov_audit_findings.description: 2 of 8 data subject access requests in Q4 2025 were completed in 35 and 42 days respectively, exceeding the GDPR 30-day response window.
gov_audit_findings.remediation_plan: Implement automated DSAR workflow with SLA tracking and escalation. Hire additional privacy analyst. Create self-service data export portal.

gov_audit_findings.title: Missing encryption for backup storage bucket
gov_audit_findings.description: One S3 backup bucket was configured without server-side encryption. Contained non-production data but violated the organization's encryption-at-rest policy.
gov_audit_findings.remediation_plan: Enabled SSE-S3 encryption on the bucket. Added Terraform policy to enforce encryption on all new S3 buckets. Scanned all buckets for compliance.

gov_audit_findings.title: Emergency change procedures not consistently followed
gov_audit_findings.description: 4 of 20 production changes in January were classified as emergency changes, but 2 lacked retroactive approval documentation within the required 24-hour window.
gov_audit_findings.remediation_plan: Add automated Slack reminder for emergency change approvals. Require approval before marking emergency change as resolved in the change management system.

gov_audit_findings.title: Vendor security assessment gap
gov_audit_findings.description: 3 newly onboarded vendors in Q4 2025 did not receive security assessments before being granted system access. Vendor management policy requires pre-access assessment.
gov_audit_findings.remediation_plan: Integrate vendor security questionnaire into procurement workflow. Block system access provisioning until assessment is approved. Retroactively assess the 3 vendors.

gov_policies.title: Information Security Policy
gov_policies.content: Establishes the organization's commitment to protecting information assets through a risk-based approach. Covers asset classification, access controls, encryption requirements, and acceptable use standards for all employees and contractors.
gov_policies.scope: All employees, contractors, and third-party vendors with access to organizational systems and data

gov_policies.title: Data Privacy Policy
gov_policies.content: Defines how the organization collects, processes, stores, and shares personal data. Covers consent management, data minimization, retention periods, cross-border transfers, and data subject rights including access, rectification, erasure, and portability.
gov_policies.scope: All personal data processed by the organization regardless of data subject location

gov_policies.title: Incident Response Policy
gov_policies.content: Defines the organization's approach to identifying, responding to, and recovering from security incidents. Includes severity classification, notification requirements, escalation procedures, evidence preservation, and post-incident review processes.
gov_policies.scope: All security incidents affecting organizational systems, data, or operations

gov_policies.title: Acceptable Use Policy
gov_policies.content: Defines acceptable use of organizational IT resources including workstations, mobile devices, email, internet access, cloud services, and social media. Covers personal use limitations, prohibited activities, and monitoring disclosure.
gov_policies.scope: All employees and contractors using organizational IT resources

gov_policies.title: Vendor Risk Management Policy
gov_policies.content: Establishes requirements for assessing, monitoring, and managing risks associated with third-party vendors. Covers vendor classification, security assessments, contractual requirements, ongoing monitoring, and offboarding procedures.
gov_policies.scope: All third-party vendors, service providers, and subcontractors with access to organizational data or systems

gov_policies.title: Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Policy
gov_policies.content: Defines the organization's strategy for maintaining business operations during disruptions. Covers recovery time objectives (RTO: 4 hours), recovery point objectives (RPO: 1 hour), backup procedures, failover testing, and communication plans.
gov_policies.scope: All critical business systems, infrastructure, and operational processes

gov_regulatory_changes.title: EU AI Act -- High-Risk AI System Requirements
gov_regulatory_changes.summary: The EU AI Act establishes mandatory requirements for high-risk AI systems including risk management, data governance, transparency, human oversight, and conformity assessments. Organizations deploying AI in the EU must classify systems and comply by August 2026.

gov_regulatory_changes.title: SEC Cybersecurity Disclosure Rules Update
gov_regulatory_changes.summary: Updated SEC rules require public companies to disclose material cybersecurity incidents within 4 business days and provide annual disclosure of cybersecurity risk management, strategy, and governance.

gov_regulatory_changes.title: NIST CSF 2.0 Framework Update
gov_regulatory_changes.summary: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 adds a Govern function, expands supply chain risk management, and improves integration with other NIST standards. Voluntary but widely adopted as compliance benchmark.

gov_regulatory_changes.title: California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA) Enforcement Update
gov_regulatory_changes.summary: California Privacy Protection Agency (CPPA) issued updated enforcement guidelines with stricter penalties for automated decision-making violations and expanded consumer rights for data correction and deletion.

gov_regulatory_changes.title: PCI DSS v4.0.1 Transition Deadline
gov_regulatory_changes.summary: All organizations handling cardholder data must fully comply with PCI DSS v4.0.1 by March 2025. Future-dated requirements for MFA enhancements and targeted risk analysis become mandatory in March 2026.
